title: Clan Invites, Mail Expiry, Pop-out Chat & Minimap Zoom
entries: Clan owners and officers can invite players by name, sent as a mailbox letter to Accept or Decline.
Most mail now expires after 5 days, including any attachments.

New optional Pop-out Channel Chat you can enable by searching 'pop-out' in Settings, then right-click the chat channel dropdown to float one channel in a draggable, resizable window that follows your main chat.
New minimap zoom on the + and - keys, plus an optional slider or buttons you can enable by searching 'zoom' in Settings.
Your rank now shows at the bottom of the hiscores when you are outside the top 50.
Right-click a player's name in chat to Add Friend, Message, or Reply.
The minimap Players toggle now cycles name and avatar, avatar only, and hidden.
Right-click your Log Basket or Ore Pouch to Fill from Bag.
The SC Shop preview slime now stops spinning to face you while you preview faces, and highlights the item you are previewing.
Other players' horses and unicorns now sit correctly on the ground.
You can now fully unbind a movement or action key (WASD, jump, crouch, map).
Rerolling Explorer contracts now turns in any finished contract first.

title: Combat Rebalance, Scribe Desk & QoL
entries: Combat rebalance: all three styles now deal even damage for their attack speed, mage spellstones scale to +60% at max tier, bows gain +3% accuracy against bosses, and Bee Ring charge costs are roughly halved.
The Scribe Desk has a new look, showing the selected scroll large beside its ingredients, plus a material picker for resource scrolls (same recipes and items).
Right-click Meteor Cores in your bank to Break Down All of them into shards without leaving the bank.
The Log Basket now sorts into the Logs tab in your bank instead of a misc tab.
You can now deposit Frenzy Bag items straight into the Washing Machine without banking them first.
Faith Orbs picked up on foot now give 6x XP, up from 4x (a 50% increase); mounted pickups are unchanged.
Quick-fire pet perk buttons grey out when you are out of charges instead of showing Ready.
The SC Shop skin preview now shows your slime alongside your chat name, and island names on the full map are readable on mobile.
You can no longer walk through meteors or cave rocks, trees, and bushes.
Performance and smoothness improvements across the pet garden, minimap, crowds, and chunk loading.
The Welcome Back offline gathering system was rebuilt behind the scenes to prepare for a bigger offline rewards buff coming soon; your current claim boxes are unchanged for now.

title: Cloud Save Reload Fix
entries: Fixed a remaining case where the "Reload Required" cloud save error could still interrupt active gatherers after quickly switching browser tabs.

title: Peer Boats, Bank Search & Enhance Buff
entries: You now see other players' boats when they sail the ocean or a shoal, tinted to their boat tier.
New search box in the Bank (on the Inventory row) filters your whole bank by item name across every tab.
SlimePower now grants more attack and defense on higher-tier gear, up to about 45% more at the top tier. Bronze is unchanged, and any gear you have already enhanced improves automatically.
Golden Sugarplum harvests now yield more fruit per patch.
Custom FaceLab faces now appear on your Adventure Log signature card.
The XP tracker now stays steady during Frenzy instead of swinging wildly. Your actual XP earned was always correct.
Fixed a bug where dying in a boss fight and getting pulled out could strand you as a ghost on an unloaded map. You now respawn cleanly at the village.

title: Pet Perk Tracker Fix
entries: Companion pet perk trackers no longer break or get stuck when you trigger Overload, Surge, Giga, or Shred with a hotkey or the perk panel instead of clicking its buff-bar icon.
Smoother performance when lots of players are gathered in one place.

title: SlimeCoin Payout Protection
entries: Added protection so big craft, cook, spin, and enchant batches always keep their full SlimeCoin payout.
Performance improvements and optimizations.

title: Mystic Orb Bug Fixes
entries: Mystic Orb bug fixes.
Continued behind-the-scenes optimizations.

title: Pet Boss Skins and SlimePower Auto-Spin
entries: Paint your pets as one of 12 boss slimes using Boss Paintbrushes: open the Pet Garden, choose a slimelet, and hit Paint.
Painted pets show their boss skin in the garden and as your walking companion, and other players can see it too.
Remove a pet's skin any time to return to a plain slime; the used paintbrush is not refunded.
New Auto button on the SlimePower panel powers an item up to a target star in one go, with an optional maximum coin spend (registered accounts only).
The AFK Slime Crab now drops a small trickle of SlimeCoins while you fight it.
Fixed a cloud save error that kept showing a Reload Required message and left some players stuck reloading.
Removed a browser permission popup that could appear when returning to the game tab.
Deposit All now skips items you have set to Pocket Bank ignore.
Mystic Slime's dialogue now closes when you walk away.

title: Living Pet Garden and Frenzy Bag Upgrades
entries: Pet Garden pets now act out more jobs around the garden.
Mystic Slime can now upgrade your Frenzy Bag from 100 up to 1,000 rewards through nine tasks.
Meat tree frenzies no longer go into the Frenzy Bag.
The Frenzy Bag, Log Basket, and Ore Pouch are now account-bound, untradable, and limited to one each.
Right-click a bag item to skip it for pets, Pocket Bank, or both.
Companion pets now sit on slopes better.
Tree models sit better on slopes, with tighter clickboxes that no longer catch the ground behind them.
Added an NPC section to the resource dropdown on the big map (M).
Sacred Grove faith circles no longer appear inside caves, boss rooms, or other instances.
Enter the Pet Garden through the new Peaceful Meadow archway.

title: Pet Garden Scroll Perks and Frenzy Bag Pickups
entries: New Pet Garden Scroll Perks panel: feed boss scrolls straight from your bag with Feed or Feed All, and see each perk's charges.
Right-click a slime in the Pet Garden to feed it just like your companion.
Frenzy rewards picked up off the ground now fill your carried Frenzy Bag, Log Basket, and Ore Pouch.
Mystic Slime now shows his portrait, and mobile tracker dragging is fixed.

title: Frenzy Bag, Session Saves, Horse Resume, and Pet Garden
entries: Mystic Slime sells the Frenzy Bag for 1,000,000 coins.
The Frenzy Bag auto-collects frenzy rewards while carried and holds 100 rewards.
Left-click the Frenzy Bag to fill it from your bag, or right-click it to empty to your bag or bank.
Banks, Deposit All, Garden Bank, and Pocket Bank empty the Frenzy Bag.
Horses now stay mounted through refreshes and short returns, but return you on foot after 1 hour away.
Sailing now resumes after reloads so ocean saves do not leave you stranded.
Improved Meteor, Shoal, and Meat Tree reward claims after mobile sleep, reloads, or tab closes.
Pet Garden has a cleaner compact panel with collect progress, shared row controls, and bottom actions.
Tap the slime or happiness bar to pet, and tap the hunger bar to feed.
Scribing pets now gather Scribing Supplies, while Hemp and Flax are Survival forage.

title: Pet Garden Jobs, Faith Orbs, and Icons
entries: Pets can now be set to Farming, Survival, Scribing, and Faith in the garden.
Farming, Survival, and Scribing pets can now bring crops, forage, materials, and scrolls to the garden pouch.
Faith pets can now bring Faith Orbs, which can be consumed for Faith XP.
Improved backend handling for the expanded pet garden rewards.
Added a Faith Orb item icon.
Refreshed Bunny Ears and Santa Hat models.
Skill tracker goals can now be set up to level 2000.
Mobile world context submenus are less likely to open off-screen.

title: Pet Trackers, Quick Fire, Frenzy, and UI Fixes
entries: Pet perk cards can be clicked to pin or stop tracker chips, with a lighter tracked highlight.
Combat Overload tracker stays in place when active time turns into cooldown, with a red active ring.
Comet Shower now arms a comet cursor; click the world to cast it, or right-click to disarm.
Pet Call no longer stacks while active, and its tracker shows how many empowered pet hits remain.
Companion scroll Feed All now handles large stacks in a batch.
Pet and potion tracker chips stay clamped on screen after refresh.
Meat Trees now play a hollow chop sound when depleted.
Prayer unlocks now update when your Faith level changes, without needing a refresh.
Skill tracker goal percent now counts from when you set the goal instead of resetting each level.
Exchange buy-offer picker now shows your real available quantity.
Horse color picker clicks work again after closing the eyedropper or color dialog.
Mobile world context menus have larger tap rows and less cramped submenus.
Classic chat layout now fits the optional send button cleanly.
Planted tree frenzy tint now reaches the full tree.

title: Farming Slimelet Trait
entries: The Cooking slimelet trait is now Farming, and equipped companions gain Farming pet XP from Farming XP.

title: Clan Customizing, Merges, and Application Letters
entries: Clan owners can now customize their clan after founding. The Customize button at the bottom of the Roster reopens the emblem builder for the shield, pattern, colors, trim, icon, and banner.
Owners can also change the clan name, tag, and motto. Emblem and motto edits are free, and changing the name or tag starts a 7-day cooldown.
Two clans can now merge. A clan owner can open another clan and propose a merge for that clan's owner to accept.
When a merge is accepted, every member joins the surviving clan, both vaults combine, and clan points add together. The joining leader becomes an officer.
You now get a letter when someone applies to your clan, and applicants get a letter when they are accepted or denied.
Clan owners can now pass leadership to another member with the Make Owner button. The old owner becomes an officer.
Founding or joining a clan now clears any applications you still had open to other clans.

title: Pet Skill Training, Companion Animations, and Mobile Controls
entries: Pet natures are now eight trainable skills: Woodcutting, Mining, Fishing, Scribing, Farming, Faith, Survival, and Explorer. Matching a pet's nature doubles its XP in that skill.
Your equipped companion now gains skill XP as you earn XP in that skill.
The pet panel header is now a live XP bar showing your companion's most recently trained skill and level.
Meat in the garden trough now keeps pets from dropping below 40 hunger and happiness while it lasts.
Garden worker pets now earn XP in the skill they gather.
The garden gather task now uses two dropdowns: pick a skill first, then pick what it gathers.
The pet customizer was restyled and now has a Trait dropdown in the save flow.
Your overworld companion now chops, mines, and casts a fishing line while you actively woodcut, mine, or fish.
Your companion floats in a life ring over deep water and stands against the tree or rock it is helping at.
Pet Call now hits for much more flat damage.
World and farming right-click menus now open their submenus on tap on touch devices.
Pet panel perk cards and timer chips now open their menus on long-press on touch devices.
Middle mouse, mouse back, and mouse forward buttons can now be bound to actions and pet abilities.
Procgen island slime statues now use a new high-contrast minimap marker that scales with zoom.
Added an Audio setting to mute your gathering sounds while a companion pet is out gathering with you (default off).
Added a Gameplay setting to turn off companion gathering animations while the pet still earns its XP (default on).

title: Pet Timers, Settings, Presets, and Targeting
entries: Right-click pet perk cards to track ready, active, or cooldown timers on the shared timer bar.
Pet perk timers can be stopped from their right-click menus and stay pinned locally across relogs.
Pet and potion timer chips keep their dragged screen position when the window is resized.
The scroll feed prompt now has Feed All for companion scrolls.
Mount/Dismount can mount your nearby horse without spending a Pocket Horse charge.
The minimap can now show a mainland meteor and a Coal Slime island signal at the same time.
PvP Cave right-click menus can now find players hidden under nearby objects and open their player card.
Gear preset tabs now show the most common washed-line emoji when saved gear has potential lines.
Potion and active buff tooltips now explain their actual effects.
Added a local setting to hide overhead protection prayer icons.
Added a Camera setting to turn off screen shake.
Added a Chat setting to hide player level-up congrats chat lines, screen announcements, or both.
Added a Chat setting to show a click-to-send button.
Other players' SlimePower enhance visuals no longer play the level-up jingle sound.
Added an Audio setting to mute other players' milestone firework sounds.
Global level 100+ messages now say Ranged and Magic instead of Bow and Mage.
Green instance banners now use black text.

title: Map Pin Fix
entries: Fixed the full-map pin control so the teleport charge no longer covers the saved-location button, and right-clicking it opens Save Location while charged.

title: Sailing, Yeti, and UI Fixes
entries: Fixed sailing resume after sleep or minimize so far-out boat positions are not pulled back toward mainland.
Frost Yeti now breaks out of the thaw animation faster after the required logs are delivered.
Slime statue circles on the minimap are now dark blue.
Companions and pet auto-loot now work inside the PvP cave.
Face mastery emote helper parts no longer pop back over custom faces.
Cave banners now say Click to Leave, and several HUD/task accents now use green.
Improved deep-sea shoal AFK limits for fully silent tabs while keeping live hidden-tab fishing intact.

title: Shoal Improvements and Bug Fixes
entries: Improved deep-sea shoal behavior so hidden, slept, killed, or reloaded tabs keep earned progress.
Deep-sea shoal AFK now uses the full shoal window and stops if you stay online out of range.
Improved hidden-tab meteor and shoal sessions so their ping/claim loop keeps running.
Improved shoal session recovery during reconnects, wakeups, focus returns, cloud loads, and PvP clears.
Fixed mainland T1 meteor map icon switching to the island/T2 marker after mining one piece.
Fixed mobile bank Inspect mode so it works on your inventory items while the bank is open.
Fixed skill tracker actions-left estimates being thrown off by Frenzy XP bursts.
Improved shoal return rewards text to say Fishing instead of Mining.
Fixed the extra Classic panel collapse button showing again.

title: Face Customization & Fixes
entries: New face customization! Buy individual eyes, cheeks, and mouth styles for your slime in the SlimeCoin shop's Face tab, and try any style on the shop preview before you buy it.
Other players now see your selected face styles.
Face mastery emotes no longer draw extra eyes over custom faces.
Half-lid and other Face tab styles now buy correctly.
Fixed equip presets losing washed potential and stars off your gear. Buffs no longer vanish when you swap presets, and affected players have had their gear restored.
Mobile bank preset submenus now stay open after long-press, so preset tabs can be selected.
Raw meats (Prime Cut, Ribeye, and Tenderloin) now stack and can be deposited to the bank like every other meat.
Service buildings near spawn (like the crafting table and spinning wheel) now have accurate click areas, so you won't trigger them when tapping a nearby fish or item.
Every boss now uses its own model as its click target, so boss clicks land where you expect.
Bonfire recipes now stay cookable as long as you have any valid ingredient, and fall back to the cheapest one you own instead of greying out.
The SlimeCoin shop no longer jumps to the top when you buy, so rapid clicking won't purchase the wrong item.
You can now claim a farming plot when your bag is full as long as the harvested crop stacks into what you're already carrying.
HP numbers now pulse when you take a hit, including misses, and stay readable longer in Classic mode.
The bank Inspect toggle now always starts off instead of being remembered on.
Moved HUD elements (XP trackers, health bars) now stay locked to where you put them when you resize the window.

title: Milestone Chat Color
entries: Milestone congrats chat broadcasts now label Game in green instead of yellow.

title: Save System Upgrade Complete
entries: Final step of the under-the-hood save-system upgrade. No gameplay changes.

title: Save System Upgrade
entries: Second step of the under-the-hood save-system upgrade. No gameplay changes.

title: Save System Groundwork
entries: Under-the-hood save-system groundwork. No gameplay changes.

title: Item System Cleanup
entries: Under-the-hood item metadata cleanup. Your items, stars, rarities, potential lines, and imbues are unchanged.
Locked item fixes.

title: Cooked Meat Icon Hotfix
entries: Cooked Ribeye and Cooked Tenderloin now use baked item icons instead of falling back while the item cache loads.

title: Apex Firework and Berry Bowl Tuning
entries: Apex Firework is a new 10 SlimeCoin one-use shop item that launches a level 2000 firework show at your position for you and nearby room peers, without posting a global congrats message.
Berry Bowl now gives 1 Cooking XP and no SlimeCoin roll, closing the cheap berry-to-cooking SlimeCoin loop.
Game/system chat messages now use a darker green style instead of the old yellow/default look.

title: Milestone Fireworks, Meat Tree Cooking, and Meteor Fixes
entries: Level 100, 300, 500, 750, 1000, 1500, and 2000 now fire milestone fireworks, nearby players can see them, and the server posts a global congrats message.
Ribeye and Tenderloin are now cookable, and Prime Cut, Ribeye, and Tenderloin can feed Washing Machine charges.
Item Lock now costs 1 SlimeCoin instead of 5.
PvP Crucible resource icons now appear on the Crucible map instead of the mainland.
Meteor mining no longer keeps earning from stale missed-stop or reload sessions.
Stale mainland meteor map blips now self-correct from the relay.
Shoal signals now mark exact spots, and island cave entrances avoid banks, birdbaths, and fountains.
Equipment presets now show lock badges on saved gear.
Right-click and long-press menus no longer leak a world click behind them.
Classic UI themes got cleaner chat, panel, inventory, and HP styling.
The Guide now covers the new cooked meats and Washing Machine notes.

title: Item System Tune-Up
entries: Under-the-hood item-system cleanup — no gameplay changes.

title: Save System Hardening
entries: Under-the-hood save-system hardening and economy safety checks.

title: Frenzy XP, Pet Scrolls, and Icon Polish
entries: Level 600+ Mining and Woodcutting frenzy XP is scaled down for long gather timers; frenzy timing and instant drops are unchanged.
Pet gathering scroll XP now scales with the pet's matching Woodcutting, Mining, or Fishing level, up to 20x at level 500.
New Self HP Bar setting: Big only, Little + big, or Little only.
Gameplay settings are grouped more clearly, and Lucky Duck is easier to click.
Baked icons refreshed for Fiber, Stormwood, Leviathan Kelp, Gooey Wood, Prismwood, and Infinity Wood.

title: Frenzy XP and Log Basket UI Fixes
entries: Mining, Woodcutting, and Fishing frenzies now award their 10x XP bonus immediately when you click the frenzy.
Slow high-level rocks and trees no longer lose frenzy XP while waiting for the next gather action.
Log Basket and Ore Pouch inventory badges now refresh their contained amount even when the inventory slot itself stays the same.
The draggable Boss Bell button now tracks drag movement more reliably, so repositioning it should no longer require repeated spam-drag attempts.
Inventory-full warnings now show a floating message on gather, farming harvest, and planted tree interactions.
The compact Sailing tracker icon was tightened so the collapsed tracker takes less space.

title: What's New Deployment Refresh
entries: Refreshed the in-game What's New deployment so the latest frenzy stack, potion timer, and Sailing tracker notes can publish correctly.

title: Frenzy Stack Fixes and Sailing Tracker Polish
entries: Mining and Woodcutting frenzies now stack their XP credits correctly on slow high-level rocks and trees.
Frenzied rocks and trees still work the normal way: click the frenzy, get the frenzy drop right away, and the 10x XP applies on the next successful gather.
Fishing frenzy was checked and remains unchanged.
Non-SlimeCoin potions now stack their timers when you drink more than one.
The Sailing tracked quest HUD can collapse to a small icon, right-click to untrack, and auto-focus quest progress again unless you manually untrack.

title: Mac Keybinds, Faith Orbs, and Nametag Menu Fixes
entries: Panel tabs now also work with number keys 1-5 by default, so Mac players do not need F1-F5 to open Inventory, Equip, Prayer, Skills, or Emotes.
Existing F-key tab binds still work, and custom keybinds are respected if a player already bound one of those number keys.
Faith orb spawning is back on the old distance method: 20 units per orb, max 5 active orbs, and the old 10-30 unit spawn range.
Faith orb pickups now give 3x XP while on foot, while horse and boat fast-travel pickup XP stays at 1x.
Removed the in-world right-click nametag style menu. Name badge styling is still available from the inventory name badge item.

title: Guide, Faith, Icons, Spider Fixes, and Polish
entries: Guide FAQ added for early money, tutorial replay, drops, combat and boss training, prayers, frenzies, pets, Faith, cosmetics, XP gear, item organization, and Washing Machine rolls.
Faith ground movement buffed: nearby orbs spawn faster with up to 15 active ground orbs; horse and boat fast travel keep the old cap.
Icons rebaked for Fiber, Koi, Elder Spellstones, jellies, trophy and deep-sea fish, Bird Nest, Sushi, bowls, pies, stews, salads, desserts, Ocean Emperor, and Abyssal Feast.
Goo Nodule, Prism Cluster, and Infinity Crystal rocks rebuilt; Goo Nodule selection now matches its larger model.
Local Settings Preset restore confirms in-panel, applies most settings live, and only reloads for anti-aliasing.
Compost Bin bulk deposits remove the right bag and bank quantities; right-click mini-map reset restores zoom and pan.
Reported bug: Spider Mage attacks could crash the fight after about a second. Fix: Spider Mage attack audio is now wired correctly.
Meteors resync after reconnects, high-tier EXP tracker action counts stay sane, and buff potion examine text shows effect and duration.
Ground item names stay sharper at low Render Scale, Meat Tree frenzies tint after chunk reloads, and tutorial, Garden Helper, and Sailing Master actions save more reliably.
Clan Activity XP-point rows show which skill XP gains earned the points, and chat history replays more reliably after sign-in or reconnect.
Horse custom colors load safely from old palettes, signature card faces export cleaner, Survival cards use the camp icon, and the emoji picker adds more reactions, ocean, space, cozy, and pet emojis.

title: Guide, Faith, Icons, and Fixes
entries: Guide FAQ added for early money, tutorial replay, drops, combat and boss training, prayers, frenzies, pets, Faith, cosmetics, XP gear, item organization, and Washing Machine rolls.
Faith ground movement buffed: nearby orbs spawn faster with up to 15 active ground orbs; horse and boat fast travel keep the old cap.
Icons rebaked for Fiber, Koi, Elder Spellstones, jellies, trophy and deep-sea fish, Bird Nest, Sushi, bowls, pies, stews, salads, desserts, Ocean Emperor, and Abyssal Feast.
Goo Nodule, Prism Cluster, and Infinity Crystal rocks rebuilt; Goo Nodule selection now matches its larger model.
Local Settings Preset restore confirms in-panel, applies most settings live, and only reloads for anti-aliasing.
Compost Bin bulk deposits remove the right bag and bank quantities; right-click mini-map reset restores zoom and pan.
Meteors resync after reconnects, high-tier EXP tracker action counts stay sane, and buff potion examine text shows effect and duration.
Ground item names stay sharper at low Render Scale, Meat Tree frenzies tint after chunk reloads, and tutorial, Garden Helper, and Sailing Master actions save more reliably.
Clan Activity XP-point rows show which skill XP gains earned the points, and chat history replays more reliably after sign-in or reconnect.
Horse custom colors load safely from old palettes, signature card faces export cleaner, Survival cards use the camp icon, and the emoji picker adds more reactions, ocean, space, cozy, and pet emojis.

title: HUD Dragging, Chat, and Meteor Fixes
entries: The Home Teleport button can now be dragged, Alt-drag resized, and Shift-right-click reset like the other HUD elements.
The Home Teleport button now follows the UI Scale slider, and a plain click still teleports.
Reset Layout now also snaps the Home Teleport button back to its default spot.
Ground item names now stay readable at low resolutions and at Render Scale below 1x.
Fixed Chat Auto-Scroll sometimes staying off after you scrolled the chat.
In the classic layout, enabling the movable coins + SlimeCoins chip no longer leaves a second chip stuck over the chat.
Dragging the tutorial and Garden Helper quest cards no longer glitches on release or saves the wrong position.
Fixed a crashed Meteor sometimes not appearing on the map or in the world until you refreshed the page.
Updated the Leviathan Geode model.

title: Quick Controls, Bank Price Check, Chat, and Combat Fixes
entries: Quick Eat is now bindable in Settings -> Keybinds.
Quick Eat supports keys, Mouse Wheel Up, and Mouse Wheel Down.
Quick Eat slot, menu, and tooltips now show your chosen bind.
Combat settings can show a draggable Quick Eat HUD button.
Home Teleport is now bindable in Settings -> Keybinds.
Home Teleport supports keys and mouse-wheel binds.
Setting a Home Teleport bind enables it automatically.
Interface settings can show a Home Teleport cooldown-ring button.
Home Teleport still follows cooldown, cast, sailing, gathering, and instance rules.
Interface settings can show a movable coins + SlimeCoins HUD chip.
Reset Layout now resets currency, tutorial, Garden Helper, and tracked task HUD positions.
Bank quantity mode now saves your 1/5/10/All choice.
Mobile bank inspect mode adds a bank-header magnifier for pinned item tooltips.
Right-clicking the Bank header opens Price Check Bank and Price Check Current Tab.
Bank price checks now show comma-formatted totals inline in the Bank header.
Bank price-check totals clear when the bank closes or opens again.
Newly crafted equipment now appears in Salvage immediately.
Washing Machine frenzy deposits now retry after a fresh save.
Washing Machine deposits now warn instead of silently failing on fresh items.
Chat settings now include Stay In Chat and Chat Auto-Scroll.
Chat channel switching snaps cleanly to the newest message.
Duplicate replayed chat messages are filtered.
Reconnect chat history is fresher.
Ground items now outline on hover by default.
Ground Item Timer Circle is a separate off-by-default setting.
Ground Item Names is a separate off-by-default setting.
Settings tester credits now include ThePunisher, Anti, and Purple.
Mini-map right-mouse drag now pans in mini mode.
Plain right-click resets the mini-map view.
Mini-map zoom-out now goes farther.
Faith, meteor, and shoal minimap markers update sooner.
Snake Boss model was remade.
Storm Vein model was updated.
Leviathan Geode model was updated.
Skele and Skele Orb party damage sync is fixed.
Party XP sharing now works for same-party players inside the same instance.
Minimized combat attack timing now matches foreground pace without burst catch-up.
Home Teleport binds no longer need a second hidden toggle.
The Bank header right-click price-check menu now opens reliably.

title: Slime Crab, Endgame Mining, Boats, and Containers
entries: Slime Crab is a new mainland AFK group combat boss with three burrows, a 30-minute timer HP bar, 50% combat XP, local claw/idle life, a small ground smash, and burrow relocation.
Inactive Slime Crab burrows crawl you to the active crab, while the active burrow no longer teleports you away.
Slime Crab peer hitsplats are optional, on by default, use a separate lavender color, and only show damage aimed at the local Slime Crab.
Cursor teleport now uses a quick flash, minimap cursor teleport uses the normal longer cast, and full map teleport still arms before firing.
Mining levels 600-1000 now have PvP Crucible nodes for Stormstone, Leviathan Pearl, Goo Core, Prism Shard, and Infinity Core.
Added Storm, Leviathan, Goo, Prism, and Infinity tools for axe, pickaxe, and fishing pole progression, with final prices of 25m, 50m, 100m, 250m, and 1b coins.
Added Storm, Leviathan, Goo, Prism, and Infinity boats with new palette-aware colors, higher boost fuel, and gentler high-tier material recipes that count loose raw materials from bag and bank.
Ore Pouch stores the new high-tier mining drops, can come from Rock Golem or Coal Miner, and upgrades with Pearls up to 500 capacity.
Log Basket now upgrades with Diamonds, and both Log Basket and Ore Pouch show colored contents counters in inventory and bank slots.

title: Saved Spots, Farm Tracker Styles, and Ocean Gift Scaling
entries: Saved Spots now stay available on the minimap even without teleport charges, support favorites, custom names, map icons, walking to a saved location, and optional auto-teleport when charged.
The minimap teleport button is back as the quick teleport arm when charges are available, and becomes a save-location pin when you have no charges.
Farm tracker slime mode now has Straw Hat Slime and Timer Slime styles, timer-outline coloring, and a label position option.
Feeding dolphins and whales now scales with Explorer level: every 100 Explorer levels lets you spend one extra Golden Fish per feed for matching stackable gifts.
Clan vault item deposits now use the correct bag-slot contract again and the vault picker layout is cleaner.

title: Daily Cave Save Hotfix
entries: Reported bug: finished daily caves could show as unfinished after an accidental refresh. Fix: cave completion writes now carry the island mapping through the existing save RPC and repair older completed rows when the client can infer the island.
Boat nametags no longer shake from vertical smoothing while sailing.

title: Nametag Polish Hotfix
entries: Peer nametags now sit lower so styled name badges stop covering remote slimes.
Remote nametag text no longer does the 1px up/down bounce while other players move.
Bright badge colors such as Fishing cyan and yellow now use crisp text instead of fuzzy colored glow.
Framed and higher name badge styles no longer shrink your own name text.

title: Multiplayer Nametag Style Hotfix
entries: Fixed multiplayer name badge styles so other players can see your selected Framed, Bottom Dash, Double Dash, and Diagonal looks when unlocked.
Reduced upgraded nametag sizing so styled name badges stay closer to the original default nametag footprint and cover less of nearby slimes.
Remote nametag updates now skip repeated DOM/style work when the displayed tag data has not changed.

title: Crypt Bell, Pet Ignore, Name Badge Styles and Safety Fixes
entries: Added a bell button to The Crypt wave HUD so stronger players can spawn the next wave immediately.
Crypt dungeon enemies now use reduced dungeon loot rates, and Meteor Shower kills in The Crypt no longer drop normal loot.
Pets now follow into The Crypt, Venom auto-loot works there, and Pet Ignore now skips matching item drops instead of only one bag slot.
Boss scrolls still grant their normal charges but now add only one pet power level per fed scroll.
Name badges now have right-click style choices, with Framed, Bottom Dash, Double Dash, and Diagonal looks unlocked by the equipped badge's own skill level.
Name badge inventory icons now show tiered styling based on your badge's skill level, and upgraded nametags now better match the tuning lab visuals in game.

Champion Ring, mastery skill capes, mastery auras, name badges, Dolphin Gift, and Whale Gift are account-bound across client drop, PvP, shop, alch, disassemble, exchange, clan-vault, and peer-loot paths.
Skill-cape perks now work from inventory where the perk exists, and Washing Machine auto-roll can target the real max legendary roll caps.

Fixed stale gear presets, PvP cave death cleanup, hostile Comet focus handling, Woodcutting Name Badge bank sorting, slope drop-text camera jerk, and procgen resource markers that could point to missing Copper Rocks.

title: Multiplayer Nameplate Fix
entries: Fixed equipped name badges not appearing on other players' multiplayer nametags.
Nameplate cosmetic changes now broadcast immediately when equipped or changed.

title: Nameplate Color and Badge Visibility Fixes
entries: Nameplate and badge players now keep a strong cosmetic accent color on their nametags.
Default nametag sizing has been restored to legacy behavior when no nameplate is equipped.

title: Nametag Sizing Fixes
entries: Fixed player nametags so the larger badge styling only applies when a nameplate cosmetic is equipped.

title: Mastery Progression, Nameplates, and Gift Flow Fixes
entries: Added multi-stage skill mastery progression for more skills, including name badges, level-500 aura rewards, and level-1000 mastery capes.
Added new Woodcutter and Hitpoints mastery progression paths in the world with dedicated houses and NPC reward flow updates.
Player nametags now show equipped nameplates and badge cosmetics more consistently.
Ocean gift claims now enforce account ownership before opening, with better failure handling and Golden Fish refunds on recoverable claim failures.
Local chat sending is now restricted to signed-in players, reducing unauthenticated local-channel spam.

title: Local Chat, Log Basket and Meat Tree Fixes
entries: Chat now has a Local channel for nearby players in the same area, with short overhead text bubbles when local messages are sent.
Log Basket now auto-empties stored logs into your bank when opening a bank or using Deposit All.
Meat Trees now keep their server phase, depleted state, and regrow timer more reliably after reconnects, chunk reloads, and bulk chopping.

title: Boss Reward Fixes and Gear Polish
entries: Reclaim Potion now can save Bee Ring melee charges as well as arrows and spellstones.
Bee Ring charge costs are lower across every melee damage setting.
Rat Shield now reduces cave fire breath damage by 75%, and its perk text matches the effect.
Skele, Rat, Spider, Wizard, and Bee bosses can now very rarely drop Reclaim Potions.
Best Gear now equips directly without overwriting an equipment preset.

title: Fishing Mastery, Ocean Gifts and Boss Drops
entries: Fishing level 1000 now has a Fisherman reward path on Slimey Knoll, with the Fishing Cape, Fishing Aura, and a Fishing Cape action that locates deep-sea shoals.

Bee Ring, Rat Shield, and Reclaim Potion are new boss rewards: Bee Ring charges with coins for melee damage settings, Rat Shield reduces cave fire breath damage by 75%, and Reclaim Potion can save arrows or spellstones.
Feeding dolphins or whales now gives account-bound Dolphin and Whale Gifts that stack in your bag and open later for server-rolled rewards.
Clan global task boards now have 6 weekly tasks with woodcutting, mining, fishing, and farming coverage.
Cooking Cape now has a 10% chance to save an ingredient set on each dish craft while worn.
Teleports keep mounted horses synced, middle-click camera drag is steadier, boat boost refills when setting sail, and the mobile minimap resource picker closes after choosing a target.
Fortune Potion now has a 25% chance to double monster drops.
Store sell prices were lowered for Storm Guava, Leviathan Mango, Sludge Papaya, Prism Kiwi, Infinity Plum, and legendary jellies from Nebula Pear Jelly through Infinity Plum Jelly.

title: Dojo Rewards and Sailing Treasure Fixes
entries: Dojo weekly reward mail is unblocked after the Monday reset, with Champion's Ring and SlimeCoin rewards still awarded once.
Sailing treasure tasks now reopen their target chest when needed, and treasure tooltips show when a chest is already opened.
Middle-click camera drag is more reliable and no longer gets stolen by browser middle-mouse behavior.
The SlimeCoin Shop remembers separate scroll positions for Cosmetics, Skins, and Consumables while the shop is open.
Task-board sign art is lighter to render, and farm patch tree order is cleaned up.

title: Hiscores and Background FPS Fixes
entries: Background FPS now throttles rendering only while the window is unfocused, so combat, gathering, timers, and XP keep normal speed.
Hiscores now load through the server leaderboard path again, so All, OG, Saplings, skill boards, and boss boards should no longer collapse to only a couple rows.
Rat, Frog, and Bee boss KC now appear in in-game Hiscore lists and clicked player profiles.
Hiscore dropdown highlights stay readable, selected category styling stays stable, and real players should no longer be mislabeled as guests.
Coconut palms now show one coconut placed higher in the crown instead of a low cluster.

title: Chunk Rendering Fix
entries: Fixed terrain chunks popping in and out as you walked, and a rare case on the lowest render distance where you could drop through the ground at a chunk edge.

title: Chef House, World Art and Performance
entries: Chef Stew now has a dedicated kitchen house, portrait, dialogue flow, and Cooking 1000 cape claim path.
The Slime Chef emote now keeps its true backflip exit for you and remote players, with cleaner cooking props, aura handling, sounds, and finale timing.
Spellstone Pool crafting now counts materials from your bank as well as your bag, and safely refunds ingredients if your materials or bag space change mid-craft.
World art and performance were refreshed across palms, docks, rocks, flax, banks, farm crops, cosmetics, and many static props, with lighter model formats and tighter chunk rendering.
Cosmetics render more consistently across lighting settings, including crowns, party hats, Bunny Ears, Santa Hat, Puffy Face, Ice Cream Cone, and Cooking Aura.
Charted island statues now use a clearer blue tint, tropical islands use the rebuilt palm trees, and the mobile combat panel takes less screen space.
Quest reward popups now show item icons or clean emoji fallbacks more reliably.

title: Desktop Layout Toggle and Cooking Polish
entries: Settings now includes Force Desktop Layout, which keeps the desktop UI on touchscreens and convertible laptops that were being put into the phone layout.
Cooking Aura and Berry Aura now register everywhere bag cosmetics need, so Cooking Aura can equip from an already claimed bag item.
The Slime Chef mastery emote has its finished backflip, dish-eating finale, visible cooked-fish toss, cooking sounds, remote-player playback, and held-tool stowing.

title: Open Travel and Actions Done Tracker
entries: Island travel tiers are open now: sailing waters, charting, teleport unlocks, and Travel map tier tabs no longer stop you by Explorer or boat tier.
Deep-sea shoals no longer require Fishing 200.
Gold fish ocean-friend gifts now make Ancient Coins much rarer, with that reward slot mostly giving pet perk scrolls and sometimes Clams.
EXP Tracker now has an Actions Done mode for counting and broadcasting session actions like logs chopped, fish caught, or hits landed.
ScapeLikes vote claims now give 3 SC.
Removed a stray bank chest near the ribbon/root island area.

title: Equipment Presets and UI Polish
entries: Clicking a slot in an equipment preset now jumps the bank to a tab with matching gear, so setting a bow, ring, or armor slot takes fewer clicks.
Best Gear, Save Worn, and Clear now ask before replacing or clearing an existing preset.
The preset editor now uses edge-to-edge shared grid lines and a cleaner Close/Equip row inside the bank.
The Emotes Favorites button now clearly shows when Favorites or add-favorite mode is active.

title: Combat Tracker, Drop Menus and Map Cleanup
entries: Combat EXP Tracker now measures EXP/hour faster during fights and keeps the last pace warm between boss kills, so dragon training spends less time stuck on measuring.
Ground item right-click menus now color item names to match their loot beam or ring, including direct item clicks and nearby Pick up rows.
Legendary ground drops now get loot beams too.
Mainland map cleanup removes extra props and resources, updates rotated port placements, and refreshes minimap resource locations.

title: Emotes, Tracker Broadcasts and Combat Polish
entries: The Emotes panel has been rebuilt with categories, search, favorites, smoother pop-out resizing, and a larger emoji set.
EXP Tracker now has a right-click Broadcast Tracker action, with chat lines for progress, EXP gained, actions left, EXP/hour, and time-left modes.
Trade windows now fit item icons and partner names more cleanly, with clearer item details for offer and inventory slots.
Shift-right-click HUD resets no longer trigger accidental movement or map clicks, including the minimap, boss bell, buff bar, and tracker.
Mobile tap-to-view item tooltips no longer flicker through hover tooltip states before settling open.
Boss, cave, and meteor visuals are cleaner: combat damage ignores already-downed states, cave mob tags clean up more reliably, and meteor effects are less obstructive in combat.
High-tier cave monsters now grant tuned combat XP more consistently across combat styles.

title: Clams, Pearls and EXP Tracking
entries: EXP Tracker now has an EXP Gained mode, with hover details and a right-click Reset EXP Session option.
Deep-sea shoals can now rarely drop Pearls, and can also drop Clams at a rarer rate.
Clams are unstackable bag items: click a Clam to open it, then click the open Clam to take the Pearl and leave an Empty Clam behind.
Pearl, Clam, Open Clam, and Empty Clam now have baked item icons and item models.
Deep-sea shoal markers now clear correctly when focus-return or keepalive data says the old shoal is gone, preventing stale discoveries after reloads or returning from another tab.
Bonfire recipe ingredient choices are more reliable when a recipe needs multiple category ingredients.

title: Spawn, Travel and Sailing Fixes
entries: Teleporting to an island, logging in, or refreshing no longer drops you underground or at sea level — you and your horse now land correctly on the terrain, even on high-elevation islands.
Treasure hunt tasks now complete when you click a chest on an island you have already looted, instead of needing a refresh.
Deep-sea shoal fishing now keeps going while you are away for much longer instead of stopping after 12 minutes.
When a deep-sea shoal's time runs out it now immediately relocates to a fresh spot instead of vanishing for 20-40 minutes.
Roaming meteors and fishing shoals now reappear correctly after your connection drops and reconnects, such as waking a backgrounded phone tab.

title: Clan Level 20 Restore
entries: Clan levels now continue to level 20, restoring Default Crown at level 10 and Royal Crown at level 20.

title: Clan Task Reset and Cap Fixes
entries: Clan global tasks with odd totals can now be completed properly, so a 537-item task no longer gets stuck after two members hit the old rounded-down cap.
Clan global tasks now reset weekly with Dojo rewards, and opening either the Clan task board or Dojo can trigger the shared weekly rollover.
Farming items can now appear in the clan task pool, and task gold and XP tuning now comes from server-side task data.
The ScapeLikes vote dot is quieter for new players and auto-enables after total level 300 unless you set it manually.

title: Tool Skins and Icon Refresh
entries: Blacksmith tier dots now let you pick any already-unlocked axe, pickaxe, or fishing rod appearance while keeping your highest unlocked speed.
The ScapeLikes vote button and claim panel were made clearer and more subtle.
The Diamond item icon was restored and the baked item icon cache was refreshed.

title: Monster Tags, Gem Polish and Reroll Banking
entries: Right-click tags are now available for cave monsters and Giant Slimes, with color and style options plus readable respawn timers.
Cap'n Bubbles can now use Uncut Gems from your bank after checking your inventory for rerolls.
The ScapeLikes social vote button was added to Settings.
Gem icons were refreshed, including the restored Diamond icon and updated rare gem art.
Registration now handles names with non-ASCII characters more reliably.

title: Farm Patch Menu Fixes
entries: Farm tree and bush patches now respect the Left Click -> Remove setting when you click the grown tree or bush itself.
Long farm patch submenus, including Quick Plant, now stay inside the screen and scroll instead of running off the bottom.

title: Enterable House and the Survival Cape
entries: There's a new enterable house on the mainland. Walk inside to meet Berry, a friendly young berry slime.
Reach Survival level 1000 and Berry will reward you with the Survival Cape and a Berry Aura. While the cape is worn or in your bag, legendary foraging finds drop three times as often.

Right-click the Survival Cape to play the new Forage emote: walk up to a wild berry bush, shake the multicolor berries into a basket, then toss it up in a berry-confetti shower. Other players can see the emote too.
The Berry Aura is a new worn effect: multicolor berries and leaves that drift and swirl around your slime.
Mobile panels are a little shorter so they leave more of the screen visible on phones.

title: Rare Gems and Companion Loot Polish
entries: New rare gemstone drops are live: Opal from Giant Slimes, Ruby from Demons, Emerald from Sandy Cave Dragons, Sapphire from Frost Cave Dragons, and Onyx from Molten Cave Dragons.
The new gems are real bag items with sell values, rarity colors, examine text, and fresh item art.
Companion slimelets can now stay with you in regular caves, and Venom auto-loot can send them toward fresh drops and collect small batches nearby.
Inventory item images fit their slots better, especially on portrait phones, and Exchange offer quantities and prices are easier to read.
Minimap marker click areas now line up better with the visible marker size, Meat Trees use their actual model shape for clicking, and a missing bush patch was added near the mainland spawn area.

title: Mobile UI, Farm Patch Menus and Spellstones
entries: Mobile inventory, equipment, and store items now use a safer first-tap tooltip flow: tap once to inspect, then tap the same item again to use, buy, or sell it.
Bank quantity menus on mobile should no longer bring up the numpad by themselves, and several update/status banners are harder to accidentally text-select.
Mobile equipment and store item icons are larger, and the portrait inventory panel has more vertical room.
Farm tree and bush patch Remove actions now walk you to the patch before removing it instead of firing from a distance.
Ready tree and bush patches now have a right-click Left Click submenu, letting you choose whether the main left click chops/picks or removes that patch type.
Farm Timer sounds now have their own Audio setting instead of sharing the Gathering Sounds toggle.
Spellstone binding now turns 1 monster catalyst plus 10 Meteor Shards into 100 Spellstones.
Tin Arrows and White Spellstones now start at +5% damage instead of +0%, with later ammo tiers stepping up from there.
The Coal Miner NPC near the coal hut has been restored.

title: Mainland Cleanup and Model Refresh
entries: Mainland object cleanup is live: several unused banks, torches, fences, old palms, rocks, mobs, and fishing spots were removed to lower object load in busy mainland areas.
Mainland ports now use the lighter island dock model instead of the heavy old port model, keeping the same service behavior with far fewer triangles.
Torches, warning signs, bank chests, workbenches, bonfires, the skull prop, and slime fountain props were optimized so common mainland decorations cost less to render.
Fishing spots use cheaper shared fish silhouettes, and their ripple rings are round again instead of looking too polygonal.
The minimap resource pins were rebaked from the cleaned world, including corrected Port and Horse Ranch entries after deleted objects were removed.
The General Store at spawn was moved back to its previous spot after the cleanup pass nudged it out of place.
Changed world models now load with the current game version in their URL, so browsers pick up refreshed docks, torches, and props after an update instead of holding stale cached models.

title: Meteor Mobile Sleep Hotfix
entries: Fixed long mobile sleeps or locked tabs while mining meteors only paying a small return payout instead of the full time earned.
Meteor mining now keeps earning through paused mobile pings until you stop mining, the meteor depletes, or the 6-hour session cap is reached.
Wake, focus, and reconnect claims now refresh the meteor session before collecting, so return-from-sleep payouts do not race the relay.

title: Combat Freeze Hotfix
entries: Fixed a crash that could freeze the game view after attacking or drawing enemy aggro, while movement and pet name labels could still appear to move.
This hotfix restores the missing animal aggro guard from the cleanup pass without changing combat balance.

title: Meteor Timer Mining and XP Potion Guard
entries: Meteor mining now settles from the server timer instead of client-side hit messages, so hidden tabs, mobile sleep, reconnects, and walking away right after returning can still claim the swings you already earned.
Meteor catch-up after a slept or backgrounded tab is less capped by old local state and now claims owed mainland and island meteor ticks through the relay.
Experience Potions now have a short 1.5-second sip guard after a successful drink, with the inventory or quick slot showing a light clockwise dark overlay while the next sip is blocked.
The Settings tester credits now include sugi and nao-ki with niwa and yuma.

title: Cleanup, Exchange Polish and Guest Drop Safety
entries: Gathered resources now use one consistent stackability policy, and the old Magic Bag upgrade has been removed from live item lists. Old Magic Bag entries are cleaned from saves.
Fresh total level displays now use the live skill list, so new HUD, admin, and remote player totals start at the correct value.
Guest sessions can no longer drop items for other players, claim player drops, or publish local ground drops to multiplayer.
Icebergs are safer to walk on again. When you step onto a floe, the surface wins over the water underneath instead of leaving you stuck inside it.
Exchange and store item hovers now use the proper equipment tooltip, with cleaner sell-offer search sizing and slimmer scrollbars.
The Hologram slime icon now matches between the SlimeCoin Shop preview and minimap/player icon, with darker face details.
Washer SlimePower numbers now sit in the bottom-left of item tiles with a readable color dot and number.
Tutorial markers for Prayer, Compost, Scribe, and Task Board now point at the live UI and world services.

title: Meteor Catch-Up and Register Fixes
entries: Meteor catch-up after a hidden, locked, or mobile-backgrounded tab is more exact. If you were already mining an Island Meteor, owed swings now settle through the server even if you walk away right after returning.
Meteor wake-up rewards are quieter on return, so catch-up creates less separate XP and drop text before the final settlement.
The Register button inside Settings now uses the same account creation path as the start screen, so guest progress, account scope, session lock, and save transfer behave consistently.
Remembered accounts get a short extra session check before being asked to log in, reducing false login prompts on slow mobile tab resumes.
New characters now keep Hitpoints level 10 correctly on first progress sync.

title: Shop, Trade and Meteor Polish
entries: The Hologram slime skin now previews correctly in the SlimeCoin Shop and uses a clearer hologram-style icon.
The SlimeCoin Shop layout is steadier on mobile, with names, prices, previews, and buy buttons staying aligned.
Trading now requires both players to be signed in, and guest accounts no longer show or accept trade actions.
Trade accept and completion feedback now appears as a centered result toast instead of a full-screen green flash.
Meteor rewards that settle after returning from a hidden or mobile backgrounded tab now combine into one compact world popup instead of a wall of stacked text.
The duplicate-tab screen now recovers sooner when an old phone tab has actually stopped responding, while still keeping Test Mode explicit.

title: Registered Name Fix
entries: Guest play now checks more than the first few matching leaderboard rows when a name belongs to a registered account, so older guest rows no longer hide the real account owner.
Registered names with older duplicate guest rows, such as accounts that played before the guest leaderboard write lock, are now reserved correctly.

title: Account Session Safety
entries: If your browser remembers a logged-in account but the session is gone, Play now pauses and asks you to log back in instead of starting a fresh guest character.
Guest play now refuses a confirmed registered account name and asks you to log in to use it.

title: AFK Gather and UI Polish
entries: Returning from a hidden, slept, or closed tab now settles active gathering immediately, including meteor and meat-tree wake retries when the world state needs a fresh sync.
Trade offers now show stack quantities correctly, make offered items disappear from your remaining inventory count, support right-click Add 1, 5, 10, X, or All, and use a cleaner completion flash.
The Washing Machine deposit list now uses the same right-click quantity menu and no longer shows the extra instruction paragraph.
The SlimeCoin Shop has a larger, cleaner preview area, clearer tabs, bigger icons and text, centered item text, and safer cosmetic preview swapping.

title: Stability and Reliability
entries: Background progress, reconnects, mobile wake, and return-from-away flows are more consistent.
Combat and boss sessions are steadier when the game is unfocused or recovering from a pause.
Exchange posting and washer actions handle stale item states more cleanly.
Backend reward accounting cleanup reduces false alarms without loosening hard protections.

title: Reliability Patch
entries: Background gathering and return-from-away handling are more reliable across long idle sessions, reconnects, and server-timed events.
This is a stability-focused update with no balance changes.

title: Teleport Animation and Fixes
entries: Companion Teleport now uses the proper Home Teleport-style cast animation for map clicks, cursor teleports, saved teleport presets, and friend teleports.
Teleporting from the full map now closes the map first, so the teleport animation is visible.
Regular daily caves now remember mounted horse state correctly, matching the existing cave and Pet Garden restore behavior.
Stormwood-tier treasure logs now sort into the Logs bank tab instead of Loot/default.
Item broadcasts now keep Boss Damage and other current potential stat lines visible.

title: Teleport Favorites, Boat Fuel and Fixes
entries: Teleport favorites are here: right-click the map Teleport arm button to save up to 5 local spots, then use them from the Teleports section while you have Teleport charges.
Teleport favorite icons are optional from that same right-click menu, and clicking a shown saved spot teleports there.
Teleport targeting can now be disarmed by clicking the arm button again, and closing the map clears targeting.
Boat boost fuel now scales by boat tier, giving higher-tier boats a longer boost meter while boost speed stays the same.
Bonfire seafood recipes now recognize newer cooked fish like Cooked Oarfish, Cooked Gulper Eel, and Cooked Anglerking in the seafood tier order.
Banks and other nearby objects stay clickable when zoomed far out on the map.
Long background or AFK graphics recovery is safer, with a restore overlay instead of leaving players on a white screen.
Session takeover reloads are safer and should no longer bounce you into the duplicate-tab warning after taking control.
Mobile long-press now opens the normal right-click action flow.

title: Farm Tracker Polish
entries: Farm tracker Pet Slime labels now render as readable tags above the slime instead of a tiny warped box.
Tracker color picking now stays contextual to the crop, bush, or tree tracker you right-clicked, and tracker slime colors no longer bleed between trackers.
Farm tracker right-click menus now stay on-screen when the tracker is near the bottom or edge of the window.

title: Friend Teleport and Farming QoL
entries: Friend Teleport can now find an online friend who is far away, instead of only working when their avatar is already nearby.
Farm tracker Pet Slime labels are now a simple Crop, Bush, or Tree toggle, with no browser pop-up naming box.
Quick Plant menus now grey out plant choices you cannot use yet because of level or seed coin cost.

title: Farm Tracker Slots
entries: Farm trackers can now keep one crop, one bush, and one tree tracker active at the same time.
Tracking another patch of the same type replaces that slot while keeping that slot's position, slime color, and optional custom name.
Right-click a tracker to rename it, close it, pick its Pet Slime color, or swap between Bar, Compact, Ring, Clock, and Pet Slime modes.

title: Island Meteor Reliability
entries: Island Meteors are now separated from regular Meteors, with Coal Miner rough hints, click-to-reveal map markers, and one shared 1,500 HP pool.
Deep Sea Shoals stay lightweight and discoverable through Coal Miner or by sailing near the live shoal.
Farm trackers can now track multiple patches at once, with Bar, Compact, Progress Ring, Countdown Clock, and per-tracker Pet Slime colors.
Bank Favorites now appear in both the bank header and the preset sidebar.

title: Island Meteor Prep
entries: Prepared client-side Island Meteor signal and visibility handling. The full reliable Island Meteor split is in v1347.

title: Shoal Signal Prep
entries: Improved Deep Sea Shoal rough signals and click-to-reveal behavior while Island Meteors were still under testing.

title: Shoals, Cape Perks & QoL
entries: Added Deep Sea Shoals, Farming Cape patch binding, Mining Cape meteor shard doubling, and improved Friend Teleport.
Cooking, farm tracking, bank/map UI, pet pouches, and several visual/animation fixes were cleaned up in this batch.
Initial Island Meteor testing started here; the reliable independent Island Meteor event is in v1347.

title: Farming QoL and Mining Mastery Rewards
entries: Farming patch QoL now includes Quick Plant for crops, trees, and berry bushes with a toggle and context-menu selection, plus right-click setup from both the crop selector and patch menus.

You can now track any farm patch directly from the patch menu; the tracker HUD mirrors XP/buff timer style and shows the patch icon, timer, and a "Done" green state, with a subtle 3-ding completion chime.
Right-click a growing crop to remove it and clear the patch quickly.
You can now scroll the Quick Plant menu to choose a seed without it closing.
The new Mining-1000 reward flow is now live with Mining Cape, Crystal Aura, and Meteor Mine emote support.
New world helpers and Coal Miner placement are available in the game world and editor.
Coal Miner now uses a baked portrait icon in the icon export cache (same format as Farmer Sprout), so mined dialogues use cached art instead of a runtime-only render.

title: Farming, Friends & QoL Fixes
entries: Farming crop sell values are now documented correctly: the high-tier crop band was lowered in the store, with Sugar Plum selling for 1,330c and Golden Sugarplum selling for 3,200c.
Golden Sugarplum also uses the intended 2-hour grow time, 0c seed cost, fixed 100 compost charge cost, 120,000 XP, and 12-20 harvest yield.
Cleaned up stale crop metadata so farming config, generated displays, and store sell values use the same numbers instead of showing old planning values.
Bank favorites now only appear in bank right-click menus, instead of being added to normal inventory item menus outside the bank.

Friend Teleport now appears for online friends in both Friends views. The teleport hotkey jumps to your current cursor target, or arms a crosshair map target when the big map is open; the map title updates while armed, and the Arm Teleport button only appears when you have charges.
If your account is already active somewhere else, the stopped-session screen now includes a safe Kick Other Sessions & Reload option that takes control and reloads fresh cloud progress.
Throwing logs into Frost Yeti braziers is much lighter now, reducing the hitch from brazier ember effects.

title: Cooking, Bank & Ocean QoL
entries: The bonfire cooking panel now lets you pick flexible ingredients, cook from your full available supply, and safely batch recipes without losing ingredients when inventory space is tight.
New cooking options are live, including Farmer Pie, Grand Farmer Pie, and legendary Jelly recipes, with recipe XP now giving fair credit for stronger ingredients.
Bank favorites now sit on the side below preset B. Right-click items in either the bank item grid or the inventory area while banking to add or remove favorites, then tap the star to filter them.
Audio settings now split sound effects into Player Combat, Boss Combat, Rare Foraging, and Legendary Drops, so rare foraging chimes and big legendary fanfares can be controlled separately.
Lucky Duck and Ocean Friend click targets are easier to select, right-click menus include their actions, and the Slime Shop preview now shows the same 2D slime icon style used on the minimap.

Trading Post buy-offer fills now cap the quantity picker to the matching items you actually own, and login/save handling is more careful when a cloud load fails instead of treating it like a fresh account.

title: DPS Calculator
entries: New DPS Calculator — open it from the small “DPS” button on your equipment panel to see your expected damage per second, max hit, accuracy, attack speed and time-to-kill against any monster, all matching the in-game combat numbers.

It reads your live worn gear and lets you experiment freely: re-roll potential lines, swap combat style, set tempo stacks, toggle prayers, buffs, potions and pet perks, pick any target, and compare two loadouts side by side — nothing you do in it ever touches your real save or gear.
Planted farm patches now use much lighter models, so having crops in view is easier on performance.

title: Performance & Crafting Refresh
entries: Big performance pass for smoother gameplay — frame rate is noticeably better while riding, in crowds, and on high render-distance or max settings, with the worst hitching from nearby slimes and hover/minimap stutter gone.
The Crafting Table has a fresh look that matches the bank, and arrow recipes now show real arrow icons with their wood and ore ingredients fully readable.

Cooking now uses your cheapest qualifying ingredient instead of your most valuable one, so batch-cooking no longer quietly burns high-tier crops like Sugar Plums, and recipe requirements now show exactly what is needed (for example 'Fruit (Stormberry+)').
Giant Rat, Bee, and Swamp Frog now have their own Slimetrics hiscore boards and show up in the Recent Boss Kills feed.
The in-game Guide now includes the three newest bosses and lists the 1-in-1000 Paintbrush cosmetic that every boss can drop.
Party XP sharing is now combat-only, so gathering keeps all of its own XP, and shared XP is capped so it can no longer power-level a low character dozens of levels from a single boss kill.

title: Combat & Woodcutting Fixes
entries: Woodcutting frenzies that land on planted tree patches can now always be collected — clicking any part of the patch picks up the frenzy instead of it sometimes feeling dead.
Clicking to attack a moving or out-of-range enemy now walks you to it and swings once you arrive, instead of standing still and swinging at where it used to be.
Fixed the Swamp Frog's punch attack so it plays its proper animation.

title: New Bosses & Farming Mastery
entries: Three new bosses are here: Giant Rat, Bee, and Swamp Frog, with new arenas, drops, guide entries, kill-count tracking, and leaderboard support.
New boss loot: Rat Cheese, Honeycomb, Frog Legs, Rat/Bee/Frog paintbrushes, new equipment rolls, and rare Bee/Frog high-tier equipment drops.
Three new boss scroll perks can now be crafted: Rat Scroll for Def Shred, Flower Scroll for Pet Call, and Swamp Scroll for Giga Mode.
Farming 1000 now has a Farmer Sprout mastery reward flow with the best-in-slot Farming Cape, Leaf Aura, Golden Sugarplums, and the Sugar Plum Master cape emote.
The Farming Cape emote can be played from the cape menu and is visible to nearby players.
Golden Sugarplums are unlocked at Farming 1000 as a premium crop with 120,000 XP, 12-20 yield, and 8,000 coin sell value.
Swamp Frog can rarely drop the Log Basket, which stores up to 100 high-level treasure logs while chopping.
Stormwood, Leviathan Kelp, Gooey Wood, Prismwood, and Infinity Wood now have faster chop cycles and doubled sell values.
Frog's final 5-fly swarm now instakills if you miss the correct prayer, and its tongue punch damage is synced to the punch animation.
Settings now has a Release Media Keys toggle so game music stops grabbing keyboard play/pause controls while sound effects keep playing.
Fixed tab-return reconnects blanking other players, fixed inventory icons waiting for an item change, and fixed new bosses giving SlimeCoins from attack rolls.

title: Bug Fix Round
entries: Flame Aura and Sparkle Aura now show up in the Slime Shop preview, so you can see them on your slime before you buy.
Sailing tasks that ask for cedar on a volcanic island now say "Burnt Logs" and point you at the burnt trees that actually drop them, instead of sending you hunting for a cedar tree that isn't there.
Mining at a safespot while the tab is in the background no longer auto-attacks an enemy that can't actually reach you, so it stops cancelling your mining.
The Adventure Book Forge stats now read "Enhance coins spent" and "Washer coins spent" instead of showing a leftover bit of code.

title: Auto-Wash & Pickup Fixes
entries: The Washing Machine can now auto-roll for you: pick the potential line you want and a minimum value, then it keeps washing on its own and stops the moment it rolls a match.
Dropped items sitting behind or under a tree can now be picked up by clicking them, instead of doing nothing and canceling your gathering.
When you tab back into the game, other players no longer disappear for a second before popping back in.

title: Crafting Fixes & Skele Speedup
entries: Spinning, cooking, scribing, enchanting, and buying in huge quantities no longer freezes or crashes the game, so giant stacks process instantly.
Scribing, enchanting, spellstone crafting, and bonfire cooking now have a small chance to award SlimeCoins, while the spin wheel's SlimeCoin rate has been reduced.
The Skele boss phase transitions are much faster now, so there is far less waiting between phases.

title: Security & Server Upgrades
entries: The SlimeCoin economy has been rebuilt to run server-side end to end, so shop purchases feel fast while staying more reliable.
A broad security pass now protects more of the economy, saves, combat, XP, item drops, PvP, the Dojo, and leaderboards.
Server, networking, save-system, database, and backend upgrades make the game sturdier as more players pile in.
You should also feel the recent improvements: more reliable SlimeCoin purchases, fixed player-to-player trades, smoother performance, and many bug fixes.

title: Performance & Stability
entries: Premium animated skins stay crisp now and no longer flicker or briefly break when other players move out of view.
Smoother performance and lower memory use over long play sessions, repeated boss fights, and back-to-back cave runs.

title: Messages Always Reach
entries: Direct messages and game invites now reach an online friend no matter where they are in the game, so a message always goes through when your friend is online.

title: Friends List Fixes
entries: Online friends in your friends list show their Game and Party invite buttons, so you can invite them straight to a mini-game or your party.
After choosing a chat channel from the dropdown, pressing Enter now drops your cursor into the chat box so you can start typing right away.

title: Friend Hub Redesign
entries: The Friends panel has a fresh look with Friends and Ignored tabs. Add a friend or someone to ignore by typing their name right in the panel, with no browser popup, and remove anyone from either list.
Reply to a direct message faster: right-click a friend's message and pick Reply, or press Tab to reply to the most recent one.
Open the Game Hub from your friends list, and use the new Back to Friends button to return to it.

title: Friend Presence & Inspect Polish
entries: Your friends list now shows who's online more reliably, with clearer status when an invite can't reach a friend right now.
Equipment comparison tooltips now show a small equals sign when Attack or Defense matches your currently equipped item.

title: Social Polish & Pet Garden Header
entries: Clan chat messages now show a small Clan badge, and recent clan chat can replay correctly after reconnecting without showing another clan's messages.
Friend direct messages now appear in chat as clean From/To friend lines instead of generic status updates.
Friends are easier to manage: the Friends channel opens the picker reliably, the Friend Hub refreshes fresh, and friend rows now use clear Msg, Game, Party, and Remove buttons.
Removing a friend, declining a request, or canceling a sent request now uses an in-game confirmation.
The Friend Hub button now flashes and shows a count badge when you have pending friend requests.
The Pet Garden panel has a bigger draggable header. The pet selector sits under it, and the slimelet color/shape customizer opens in the top empty space above the portrait.

title: Friends, Clan Chat & Party XP
entries: New friends list and ignore list, tied to your account. Right-click a player to add or ignore, or add a friend by name from the Friends panel. Mutual requests auto-accept.
Clan chat works now and reaches only your clan.
The chat dropdown has a new Friends option: see your friends and send private messages, with replies shown right in chat.
Invite a friend straight into a game from your friends list, or open the Game Hub from there.
Party XP sharing: party members in the same area share experience. You keep most of yours, the rest is split nearby, and you never lose XP when you are on your own.

Fixes: rare drops land in the open near you instead of inside hills or bushes and right-click Pick up walks you over to grab it, resizing a popped-out panel like Emotes is smooth, meteors no longer get stuck after locking your phone mid-mine on mobile, and Roots are easier to click.

title: Respawn Hotfix
entries: Fixed deaths where the ghost could fade out before you clicked Respawn in boss, dungeon, or PvP-style death states.
Refreshed the Garden Helper changelog wording so it describes the final release cleanly instead of an internal tuning pass.

title: Garden Helper, Respawn Choice & Crafting Feedback
entries: New Garden Helper quest: Farmer Sprout now walks you through the Pet Garden with dialogue, quest markers, highlighted buttons, and rewards. If you already have a slimelet, he recognizes it and skips the forced hatch.

Garden onboarding is smoother: the first slimelet is free, Sprout gives 100 Tree Meat, Meat Trees unlock at Woodcutting 30 with level-scaled XP, and Sprout gives you a small starter scroll to try the system.
Farmer Sprout has a proper baked portrait and a cleaner dialogue panel with the portrait on the left and edge-to-edge dialogue controls.
The shared pouch step now highlights Collect and lets you press it even if the pouch is empty, so you learn the action without waiting for +1 item.
Crafting now shows a lightweight result toast inside the crafting UI with the crafted item icon and rarity, without closing your inventory.
Deaths now wait for you to click Respawn instead of instantly snapping you back.

Fixes and polish: party boss entry crashes are fixed, stacked mobs can be chosen from the right-click menu, mailbox sale payouts can show which items sold, buff rings were polished, prayer tooltips no longer stick, Rat Slimes are back near the early mainland path, and Garden music was updated.

title: Combat & Drop Fixes
entries: Minimized AFK combat no longer wanders off to attack peaceful animals like cats, and giant slimes can no longer path through safespots while your game tab is in the background.
Rare fruit drops such as Sun Peach now show a clean center-screen popup instead of a floating label that could streak across the screen and look like a camera jerk at certain camera angles.

title: HUD Ring Polish
entries: The buff and boost timer ring now matches the XP tracker ring. It is the same size by default and drains clockwise the same way.
In the compact ring, the number now sits centered directly under the skill icon, with the percent sign tucked neatly beside it.

title: Pet Garden Overhaul, HUD Customization & New Fruits
entries: The Pet Garden is now a shared space. You'll meet other players there, see their slimelets wander in and out through a new cave entrance, and you can click one to pet it.

Plant in the garden: Tree and Bush patches are real farm patches now, and the new Magic Patch takes Magic Seeds. Each seed keeps your pets fed (full hunger) for 10 hours, so they stay effective. The crew still needs Tree Meat in the trough to gather.
The garden now has its own Bank Chest, you can roam much farther out into the meadow, and you keep your horse after boss, dungeon, and PvP runs instead of losing it.
The slimelet collection popup now shows item icons in a cleaner layout.

Make the HUD yours: drag any HUD piece to move it, hold Alt and drag to resize it, and Shift plus right-click to reset it to default position and size. Works on the HP bar, boss bar, buff bar, XP tracker, and the leave banner.
The buff/boost bar has a fresh look with round timer rings. Right-click a buff to change its ring style, color, font, or size.

The XP tracker has a new 'Compact Ring (Level)' mode and is colored by skill, the buff bar and XP tracker swapped default spots, there's a new 'Hide Game Hub Button' setting, and 'Reset UI to Default' now snaps everything back instantly.
Five new legendary fruits drop from the high-tier berry bushes: Storm Guava, Leviathan Mango, Sludge Papaya, Prism Kiwi, and Infinity Plum. Each has a new bonfire dish to cook.
Binding Spellstones is much cheaper: 1 Meteor Shard plus 1 catalyst now makes 10 Spellstones.
Chat polish: clearing chat sticks after reconnecting, you can right-click to copy selected chat text, and right-click a chat box to paste.

title: Meteor Frenzy Glow-Up, PvP Polish & Fixes
entries: Meteor frenzy is a spectacle now: the meteor glows molten-orange while it lasts, you earn 10x XP, and you scoop up a burst of bonus Meteor Shards.
Break down Meteor Cores: right-click a Meteor Core to turn each one into 10 Meteor Shards for binding Spellstones.
PvP in the Crucible now attacks at a smooth, continuous speed that matches your normal PvM rhythm - the old start-stop stutter is gone, and damage is unchanged.
On mobile, reconnecting after your phone has been locked for a while now brings other players back onto the map and minimap right away, instead of showing an empty world while you appear online.
Spellstones now show their proper color names everywhere in the Spellstone Pool menu and its status messages.
Shoreline foam now lines up cleanly with the water when viewed from a distance, and Torches are properly lit and shaded to match the world.

title: World Streaming, Slime Restyle & Smoother Play
entries: New World Streaming 'Edge Reveal' (Settings - Camera): the world becomes a clean slab that slides with you, with sky beyond the edge - on by default (Circle) and sized to show your whole render-distance area.
Restyle your slimelets: recolor and reshape any slimelet right from the Pet Garden. Pets also get hungry and unhappy far more slowly, and the Pet Treat costs less.
Smoother camera panning and lighter distant water, plus scroll icons are now tinted by family and tier so they are easy to tell apart.
New 'Hide Combat Style Panel' setting, ground items are bigger and sit on the ground, Spellstones now have clear color names, and Home Teleport works while you are gathering.
Home Teleport and dropped items now save right away - teleporting or dropping something and quickly refreshing no longer sends you back to the old spot or returns the item.
Fixed false 'logged in from another browser' and 'Reload Required - progress continued while asleep' warnings that could interrupt normal single-device and AFK play.
PvP in the Crucible feels tighter - your hit splat appears the instant you swing, you attack at your normal speed, and damage is unchanged; also fixed PvP damage on guest accounts.
Fixes: a rare graphics console error is gone, and these patch notes use a clearer, larger font.

title: Minimized Combat, UI Refresh & Fixes
entries: Combat now keeps going while the game is minimized — your slime keeps fighting and chains kills as monsters respawn, for all styles, with sounds. It runs at normal speed with no bonus XP, for up to 15 minutes after your last input.
Monsters now make a soft little grunt when they die.
Fixed some monsters (like raccoons and wolves) being counted twice — which doubled their loot drops and the damage they dealt you. Also fixed a double-swing when tabbing back in.
Farm bushes now grow into the right plant — berry and Voidberry bushes were showing wrong or invisible models.
The Bank and General Store have a clean new edge-to-edge look. The Farm and Compost windows no longer overflow the screen.
World Map: picking a resource found in the PvP cave now pings the cave entrance.
Nights are smoother and warmer — less red with a gentler glow. Torches, campfires and bonfires now share one improved flame.
The day/night cycle is optimized for no stutter, and its extra night detail (stars, clouds and the sunset glow) scales to your graphics quality.
The Ghost Slime spot is back, in the world and on the map.
Security: PvP damage is capped by your real, server-verified level to stop level-spoof one-shots, bounties are set by the server, and being kicked no longer locks you out.

title: Day/Night Cycle, Torches, Iceberg & Bush Patches
entries: New Day/Night Cycle: the overworld sky now moves through a full day on your local clock — sunrise and sunset color shifts, the sun arcing and setting behind the hills, a moon, twinkling stars and drifting clouds, with nights gently dimming the world. On by default; toggle it in Camera settings (Day/Night Cycle).
Torches you can place light up at night: ambient torches around the world now glow and flicker after dark for cozier night-time exploring.
New floating Iceberg: walk out onto the drifting ice floe off the coast — it gently rocks and tips toward whichever side everyone crowds onto.
